summary: Systems re-review accepts SIM-003. The prior Query v1/StateDelta alias leak is fixed: packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts now copies DistrictControlState at the world-state, delta, and query read-model boundaries, and tests/sim-core-command-query.test.ts includes regression coverage proving mutation of returned delta/query control DTOs cannot mutate runtime.world or invalidate WorldState invariants. GameCommand v1 remains versioned and minimal for M1, player/AI/system actors share the same validator and apply path, expected failures remain structured, adapters delegate to sim-core, and Node/Worker command-query hashes match.

tests_run.result: After booting minimal-m1, applying debug.set-district-control, mutating the returned state.district-control-updated delta control to 777, querying sim.list-district-summaries, and mutating the query control to 999, runtime.world.state.districts[0].control.controllerPolityId remained 1 and validateWorldStateV0(runtime.world) returned no errors.
Vitest passed: Test Files 5 passed (5), Tests 27 passed (27). Includes alias isolation regression for Query v1 and StateDelta district control DTOs.
Vitest passed: Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 3 passed (3).
Node hash 61e0ba1c matched Worker-compatible hash 61e0ba1c; Node command/query hash d275faa4 matched Worker-compatible command/query hash d275faa4.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.
architecture:check and sim:determinism passed; root Vitest Test Files 8 passed (8), Tests 33 passed (33); web Vitest Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 3 passed (3).
pnpm -r --if-present typecheck completed successfully across workspace packages with typecheck scripts.
ESLint completed with no reported errors.
All matched files use Prettier code style.

decisions: Accepted the explicit copyDistrictControlState boundary helper as the minimal M1 fix; no new abstraction or ADR is required.
Accepted runtime-scoped duplicate command detection for M1 with the writer-documented follow-up risk for snapshot/save v1 persistence.
risks: Future nested read-model DTOs need the same explicit copy-at-boundary treatment to avoid reintroducing object aliasing.
Duplicate command tracking is still runtime-scoped and should be revisited with snapshot/save v1 work.
requested_action: Route SIM-003 to the next required review or closure step; systems_architect accepts the command/query v1 architecture after the alias isolation fix.
